title: Fix: callout text unreadable in dark mode (light on light)
description: Callout boxes (.warn, .tip, .note) show unreadable text in dark mode when the dark-mode background overrides are missing from a generated document. Root cause: the callout rule uses inherited text color (--cm), which goes light-gray (#d6d3d1) in dark mode, but the background may still be the light pastel (#fef3c7, #dcfce7). Fix: add color:var(--ct) explicitly to the .note,.warn,.tip rule in the base CSS block. --ct correctly inverts between modes: dark (#1c1917) in light mode on light pastels, light (#fafaf9) in dark mode on dark pastels.
close_reason: Fixed: added color:var(--ct) to .note,.warn,.tip rule in base CSS block

title: Strengthen markup subagent prompt with compression-first instructions
description: The current Haiku subagent prompt tells it to use compressed markup but doesn't enforce it strongly enough. The agent still produces readable, indented output by default. Update the MARKUP SUBAGENT PROMPT section in SKILL.md to: (1) explicitly list which HTML closing tags to omit, (2) require no whitespace between tags in the output, (3) ban HTML indentation, (4) ban comments, (5) require CSS shorthand throughout, (6) add a concrete before/after example showing uncompressed vs compressed output so the agent calibrates correctly.

title: Write explicit markup compression rules into SKILL.md
description: The skill says 'compressed markup' but doesn't enumerate what that means precisely. Agents still default to readable, indented HTML. Add a dedicated 'Markup Compression Rules' section to the skill with explicit, exhaustive rules an LLM can follow: omit optional closing tags (p, li, td, tr, th, thead, tbody), omit quotes around simple attribute values, no whitespace between tags, no indentation, no HTML comments, use boolean attributes without values. Humans never read the source; only agents and LLMs do. The rendered output is what humans see.

title: Add 7 missing high-value component patterns
description: Components that will be needed constantly but aren't in the skill: (1) stat block — large metric + label for dashboards; (2) timeline — chronological event sequence; (3) numbered procedure — visual steps with circle numbers; (4) before/after split — two-column comparison layout; (5) details/summary — native collapsible, no JS needed; (6) diff table — red/green rows for changelogs and migrations; (7) table of contents — auto-generated from h2 headings, ~15 lines of JS. Each is 10-25 lines of HTML/CSS.
close_reason: Added 7 new component patterns to SKILL.md: stat block, timeline, numbered procedure, before/after split, collapsible section, diff table, and table of contents

title: Build Content Plan template library for 8 common document types
description: Agents generating incident reports, ADRs, sprint retros from scratch produce inconsistent output every time. Pre-built Content Plan templates for the most common types would dramatically improve consistency and speed: incident report, architecture decision record, sprint retrospective, research brief, onboarding guide, API reference page, project proposal, agent session-close summary. Each template = pre-filled DOCUMENT/AUDIENCE/PURPOSE/ACCENT + section scaffolding.
close_reason: Added 8 Content Plan templates to SKILL.md: incident report, ADR, sprint retro, research brief, onboarding guide, API reference, project proposal, agent session-close summary

title: Preserve Content Plan as embedded artifact in generated HTML
description: The Content Plan produced in Phase 1 is thrown away after Haiku generates the HTML. Embedding it in <script type='application/floreo' id='content-plan'> enables: future agents to update docs without reverse-engineering markup, multi-format export (markdown, PDF), and document understanding without opening the file. Define the embedding convention and update the update protocol to use it.
close_reason: Added content plan preservation convention: embed in <script type='application/floreo' id='content-plan'> before </body>; updated Haiku prompt (req #11), update protocol step 1, and quality checklist

title: Fix 5 specification gaps in floreo SKILL.md
description:
1. Haiku prompt has unfilled [paste...] placeholders
2. Content Plan table section format underspecified (add worked example)
3. No file naming/output location guidance
4. No lower bound - when NOT to invoke floreo
5. No update protocol for existing floreo documents
close_reason: Fixed all 5 spec gaps: Haiku prompt placeholder clarified with angle-bracket convention + warning, Content Plan table/figure format with worked examples, file naming convention + docs/ default, min threshold (2-of-4 rule) + explicit do-not-use list, update protocol (read→plan→regenerate→v2 suffix)

title: Fix 5 CSS bugs in floreo SKILL.md
description:
1. Table border-radius broken by border-collapse (move border/radius/overflow to .tbl-w)
2. Hardcoded colors in .warn/.tip violate CSS variable rule (add --cw-bg/--cw/--cg-bg/--cg tokens)
3. Spacing vars --s1-s8 documented but missing from :root block
4. Type scale vars --t-xs through --t-4xl documented but missing from :root
5. .eyebrow and .subtitle referenced in skeleton but have no styles
close_reason: Fixed all 5 CSS bugs: border-radius/border-collapse split to .tbl-w, warn/tip hardcoded colors → --cw/--cg tokens, .eyebrow/.subtitle added, spacing/type scale vars in :root, code block pre-wrap

title: Build floreo skill SKILL.md
description: Create the core floreo skill file that instructs agents to produce beautiful self-contained HTML documentation. Covers philosophy, HTML structure, CSS system, SVG, JS, and two-phase Haiku orchestration.
close_reason: Created skills/floreo/SKILL.md with full skill spec: HTML philosophy, two-phase Haiku orchestration, design system, CSS token block, SVG/JS guidelines, component library, and Haiku subagent prompt template

title: feat: sticky sidebar ToC on wide viewports
description: On desktop viewports (≥1100px), the ToC should move into a right-hand sidebar column alongside the main content column, and stick to the top of the viewport on scroll so it remains accessible regardless of scroll depth. On smaller viewports, the ToC drops inline below the header as it does today. This requires: (1) a two-column CSS grid layout for wide screens with main content in the left column and ToC in the right, (2) position:sticky on the ToC nav, (3) a JS update to active-link tracking so the sidebar ToC highlights the currently-visible section. Must remain self-contained (no external CSS/JS).
close_reason: ToC now renders as sticky right-sidebar on ≥1100px viewports via main grid layout; active-link tracking via IntersectionObserver added to JS

title: Add file size budget to quality checklist and output rules
description: The skill mentions 100KB max but it's buried in the HTML Philosophy section and not enforced in the quality checklist. Move it into the quality checklist as an explicit gate. Also define per-template budgets: simple docs (report, retro, session-close) should target <30KB; complex docs with SVG charts (incident, proposal, research brief) should target <60KB. Add these as checklist items so agents flag before writing when a document is approaching the limit.

title: Shorten remaining verbose class names in the component library
description: Some component classes are still longer than necessary. Review the full class name inventory: .steps/.step/.step-n/.step-bd, .stats/.stat/.stat-n/.stat-l, .toc/.toc-item, .timeline/.tl-item/.tl-dot/.tl-body, .d-add/.d-rem/.d-chg. Propose shorter aliases and update the base CSS block, component HTML examples, and markup subagent prompt in SKILL.md. A class like .step-bd could be .sbd; .stat-n could be .sn. Goal: no class name longer than 4 chars where possible.
close_reason: 24 class groups shortened in SKILL.md: .eyebrow→.ey, .subtitle→.sub, .tbl-w→.tw, .stat-n→.sn, .stat-l→.sl, .step-bd→.sbd, .step-n→.spn, .steps→.sps, .timeline→.tl, .tl-item→.tli, .tl-dot→.tld, .tl-time→.tlt, .toc-hd→.toch, .toc-list→.tocl, .collapsible→.clp, .collapsible-hd→.clph, .collapsible-bd→.clpb, .split→.spl, .split-lbl→.slbl, .split-before→.slb, .split-after→.sla, .d-add→.da, .d-rem→.dr, .d-chg→.dc. Base CSS, component examples, and subagent prompt all updated.

title: Audit floreo output: baseline size measurements per template type
description: Before optimizing, establish what we're working with. Generate one example document for each of the 8 floreo template types (report, incident, ADR, retro, research brief, proposal, agent brief, session-close) and measure the file size of each. Record in a table: template type, line count, byte size, CSS share, HTML share. This gives us a baseline to measure optimization wins against and helps identify which templates are heaviest.
close_reason: Baseline generated for all 7 missing template types (incident, ADR, retro, onboarding, API ref, proposal, agent-brief). Sizes: 5.6-7.3KB minimal (CSS overhead=3.4KB fixed). Real-world: session-close=14KB, research-brief=27KB. Results fed into vu2 budgets. Baseline files in docs/baseline/.

title: Define floreo session-close self-report as a first-class document type
description: After complex tasks, agents should write a structured floreo summary: what was done, key decisions made and why, what was uncertain, what context would help a future agent continue. Currently these are markdown paragraphs or nothing. A floreo self-report would be scannable, searchable, and consistently structured. Define the template and integrate it into the session-close protocol as a standard output.

title: Add machine-readable floreo metadata to document head
description: Every floreo doc should embed structured metadata as <meta name='floreo:*'> tags: type, created timestamp, creating agent/model, version number. Enables: agent-based document discovery via grep, automated cataloging, document registry tooling. Zero cost to add now; valuable infrastructure for any future tooling.

title: Fix SVG marker ID collision in multi-figure documents
description: The flow diagram pattern uses id='arr' for arrowhead markers. A document with multiple flow diagrams gets multiple id='arr' attributes — invalid HTML, and browsers use only the first. Fix: unique IDs per figure (arr-1, arr-2) OR define a single shared <defs> block once at the top of the body and reference it from all figures.

title: Add dark mode via prefers-color-scheme to base CSS
description: The design system is light-only. Dark mode is the default environment for many users, especially developers. Add a @media (prefers-color-scheme:dark) block to the base CSS with token overrides (--cb, --cs, --ct, --cbr). Must be added proactively — retrofitting dozens of generated docs later is much harder.

title: Define floreo brief format for multi-agent context passing
description: Agents in multi-agent pipelines pass context as JSON or plain text. Floreo HTML could be richer: a 'brief' one agent produces and another reads. HTML structure makes it easy to extract specific sections programmatically (querySelector) while humans can review the same file in a browser. Define conventions for brief-type documents: compact format, structured sections (objective, constraints, context, handoff notes), <meta floreo:type='brief'>.

title: Add data-floreo-id convention for section-level diffing and versioning
description: HTML diffs are unreadable. Adding data-floreo-id='section-slug' to each <section> element enables a floreo-aware diff to show 'Section: risk-analysis changed, Section: timeline added' instead of raw line diffs. Also enables stable cross-references between documents (link to a specific section by stable ID across versions). Zero cost to add the convention now; unlock tooling later.

title: Define floreo reading interface: ToC, print, search, export
description: A ~100-line optional JS+CSS block that turns floreo docs into light reading apps: sticky table of contents that tracks scroll position; print-optimized @media print styles; in-document text search with highlight; 'copy as markdown' export. Opt-in pattern added to document-specific JS section, not the base block. Makes floreo feel like a product, not a file.

title: Prevent visual monotony in large floreo document collections
description: Five accent colors for five document types means all Technical docs look identical. At scale (30+ docs/month) a team's floreo library is visually indistinguishable. Need: a broader accent palette with variants, OR a project-level brand color mechanism (read from CLAUDE.md or project config), OR layout variation guidance beyond the one fixed template.

title: Add file size threshold and escape hatch for single-file rule
description: Large SVGs, embedded fonts, and base64 images can push floreo docs past 200KB. At scale this creates git bloat and slow loads. Need a size threshold (~100KB) and explicit guidance on when/how to break the single-file rule for technical reasons, and what to do when you do break it.

title: Decouple Phase 2 model from Haiku-specific name
description: The skill says 'spawn a Haiku subagent' which bakes in today's model hierarchy. When a smaller/cheaper model ships, pricing changes, or Haiku is unavailable, the guidance becomes wrong. Should express the principle ('most efficient model sufficient for mechanical markup') with a current example in parentheses.
